lede: A look at August 27 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Ariana Greenblatt and Sulley Muntari, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Hurricane Irene
events.desc: Hurricane Irene strikes the United States east coast, killing 47 and causing an estimated $15.6 billion in damage.

events.title: Comair Flight 5191
events.desc: Comair Flight 5191 crashes on takeoff from Blue Grass Airport in Lexington, Kentucky, bound for Hartsfield–Jackson Atlanta International Airport in Atlanta. Of the passengers and crew, 49 of 50 are confirmed dead in the hours following the crash.

events.title: The Troubles
events.desc: The Troubles: Eighteen British soldiers are killed at the Warrenpoint ambush by the Provisional Irish Republican Army near Warrenpoint, Northern Ireland, in the deadliest attack on British forces during Operation Banner. An IRA bomb also kills British royal family member Lord Mountbatten and three others on his boat at Mullaghmore, Republic of Ireland.

events.title: Calder Hall nuclear power station
events.desc: The nuclear power station at Calder Hall in the United Kingdom was connected to the national power grid becoming the world's first commercial nuclear power station to generate electricity on an industrial scale.

events.title: Guinness World Records
events.desc: The first edition of the Guinness Book of Records is published in Great Britain.

events.title: Anglo-Zanzibar War
events.desc: Anglo-Zanzibar War: The shortest war in world history (09:02 to 09:40), between the United Kingdom and Zanzibar.

events.title: 1883 eruption of Krakatoa
events.desc: Eruption of Krakatoa: Four enormous explosions almost completely destroy the island of Krakatoa and cause years of climate change.

events.title: Black Hawk (Sauk leader)
events.desc: Black Hawk, leader of the Sauk tribe of Native Americans, surrenders to U.S. authorities, ending the Black Hawk War.

events.title: Napoleonic Wars
events.desc: Napoleonic Wars: The French Navy defeats the British Royal Navy, preventing them from taking the harbour of Grand Port on Île de France.

events.title: Sack of Rome (410)
events.desc: The sacking of Rome by the Visigoths ends after three days.

births.name: James Molyneaux, Baron Molyneaux of Killead

deaths.text: Stevie Ray Vaughan, American singer-songwriter, guitarist, and producer
Ernest Lawrence, American physicist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate
Eise Eisinga, Dutch astronomer and academic, built the Eisinga Planetarium
